# Sonic Visualizer Knowledge Base for Dance Hit Audio Signature System
# This file maps Sonic Visualizer concepts to our 8-dimension analysis framework.

## Sonic Visualizer Overview
Sonic Visualizer is an open-source application for viewing and analyzing audio files.
It supports VAMP audio analysis plugins for feature extraction.
Website: https://www.sonicvisualiser.org/

## VAMP Plugin Equivalences to Our 8 Dimensions

### Dimension 1: BPM Stability
- VAMP Plugin: vamp-example-plugins:fixedtempo
- SV Layer: Time Instants (beat positions)
- Our Method: Autocorrelation-based tempo tracking, stability = 1/variance
- SV Equivalent: Queen Mary Tempo Tracker (qm-vamp-plugins:qm-tempotracker)

### Dimension 2: Bass Energy
- VAMP Plugin: Not directly available as single plugin
- SV Layer: Spectrogram (narrow band, 20-250 Hz)
- Our Method: FFT-based bandpass + RMS energy in 20-250 Hz band
- SV Workflow: Use spectrogram with frequency range locked to bass band

### Dimension 3: Vocal Presence
- VAMP Plugin: Melodia (melody extraction in vocal range)
- SV Layer: Time-Value (spectral centroid)
- Our Method: Spectral centroid + HNR in 300-3400 Hz
- SV Equivalent: vamp-libxtract:spectral_centroid + manual band filtering

### Dimension 4: Beat Strength
- VAMP Plugin: qm-vamp-plugins:qm-onsetdetector
- SV Layer: Time Instants (onset positions) + Note (onset strength)
- Our Method: Onset detection via spectral flux, envelope peak amplitude
- SV Equivalent: Queen Mary Onset Detector strength output

### Dimension 5: Spectral Flux
- VAMP Plugin: vamp-libxtract:spectral_flux (if available)
- SV Layer: Time-Value (spectral flux curve)
- Our Method: Frame-to-frame spectral magnitude difference (Euclidean)
- SV Equivalent: Can be visualized as time-value layer from onset detection

### Dimension 6: Rhythm Complexity
- VAMP Plugin: qm-vamp-plugins:qm-barbeattracker (beat grid)
- SV Layer: Time Instants (beat + sub-beat positions)
- Our Method: Onset density + syncopation index (IOI coefficient of variation)
- SV Workflow: Compare onset times against quantized beat grid to measure syncopation

### Dimension 7: Harmonic Richness
- VAMP Plugin: nnls-chroma:chordino (chord detection)
- SV Layer: Region (chord regions) + Spectrogram
- Our Method: Harmonic peak count + spectral flatness
- SV Equivalent: NNLS Chroma + spectral_flatness from libxtract

### Dimension 8: Dynamic Range
- VAMP Plugin: vamp-libxtract:rms_amplitude
- SV Layer: Time-Value (amplitude envelope)
- Our Method: Crest factor (peak/RMS) per segment
- SV Workflow: Compare peak waveform against RMS envelope

## Sonic Visualizer Workflow for Validation
1. Open audio file in Sonic Visualizer
2. Add spectrogram pane (Pane > Add Spectrogram)
3. For each dimension, add the corresponding VAMP plugin:
   - Layer > Add Analysis Layer > select plugin
4. Compare visual output with our MATLAB heatmap
5. Use SV's measurement tool to verify specific time segments

## Key SV Settings for Dance Music Analysis
- FFT Size: 2048 (matches our config.fftSize)
- Window: Hann (matches our windowing)
- Hop Size: 512 (our hop is windowSamples * 0.5)
- Frequency Scale: Log (for visualizing bass vs vocal ranges)
- Color Scale: dB (for energy visualization)

## Useful VAMP Plugins for Dance Music
- qm-vamp-plugins: Queen Mary plugins (tempo, onset, beat tracking)
- nnls-chroma: Harmonic analysis (chroma, chord detection)
- vamp-libxtract: Low-level feature extraction
- bbc-vamp-plugins: Rhythm and spectral features
- Melodia: Vocal melody extraction
